page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX-512_VNNI, AES, and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks indicate a mix of average and weak results. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and gzip compression single-threaded benchmarks scoring in the average tier. However, multi-core scaling is limited, as shown by weak ratings in stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth also presents a performance bottleneck, with both cached small-block and uncached large-block read and write operations falling into the bottom 25 percent tier. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET operations show weak throughput, while static web serving throughput remains in the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g7nex.xlarge offers a dedicated CPU allocation at a standard general-purpose resource density. The lack of bundled local storage and the weak multi-threaded performance suggest that this instance is best suited for workloads that do not demand high parallel processing or intensive memory throughput. Appropriate workloads include moderate web serving, single-threaded application tasks, basic microservices, and lightweight development or testing environments that benefit from dedicated CPU resources without requiring high-performance multi-core execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8369B processor under the KVM hypervisor. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput, but weak multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth. The instance supports advanced instructions like AVX-512 and AES. Given its qualitative cost profile as a dedicated resource with moderate single-threaded capabilities but limited parallel processing power, it is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as moderate web serving, basic microservices, and single-threaded application tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for moderate web serving and single-threaded tasks.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.xlarge features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, delivering balanced general-purpose performance.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance offering balanced compute and memory resources for moderate single-threaded workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Average single-core CPU and static web serving benchmark performance
Suited for moderate web serving, microservices, and single-threaded tasks
